page: Amazon Web Services c6id.32xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores from an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, supported by 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per physical core. Local storage is bundled, consisting of 7600 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes. Network connectivity is established with a baseline bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core CPU and Passmark CPU scores placing in the top 10% of tested systems. The processor's support for AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration drives high throughput in extended instruction tests. Single-core performance falls in the average tier, indicating that the instance's primary strength lies in parallelized execution. Memory bandwidth is strong, reaching 332.31 GB/sec for large block read-only operations, while static web serving throughput reaches 68.84 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c6id.32xlarge offers high compute density and massive local NVMe storage without requiring external block storage attachments, optimizing resource utilization for storage-heavy compute workloads. The combination of high-speed local NVMe SSDs, 50 Gbps networking, and 128 vCPUs makes this instance highly suitable for data-intensive applications, high-performance web serving, distributed databases, and multi-threaded batch processing.
description: Amazon Web Services c6id.32xlarge is a compute-optimized instance designed for high-throughput parallel workloads. Powered by the Nitro hypervisor, it features 128 vCPUs and 64 physical Intel Xeon 8375C cores running at 3.5 GHz on an x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory and a substantial 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. In benchmark evaluations, the instance delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 10% for stress-ng and Passmark CPU tests, alongside strong memory bandwidth and static web serving throughput. While single-core performance is average, its massive parallel processing capabilities and high-speed local storage provide excellent qualitative cost efficiency for data-intensive operations. The c6id.32xlarge is highly suited for distributed databases, multi-threaded batch processing, and high-performance web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6id.32xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 128 vCPUs, 256 GB DDR4 RAM, and 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier multi-core performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c6id.32xlarge features 128 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 7600 GB NVMe SSD storage, optimized for high-performance parallel workloads.
tagline: A high-core-count compute instance featuring massive local NVMe storage and top-tier multi-threaded performance for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 128 vCPUs and 64 physical Intel Xeon 8375C cores at 3.5 GHz
Equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance and strong memory bandwidth
Features 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth and Nitro hypervisor integration
Best suited for distributed databases, high-performance web serving, and batch processing
